title: helianthella scabra
intro: Helianthella scabra, also known as rough helianthella, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub-like perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Herbal preparations of Helianthella scabra typically involve drying and infusing the plant's leaves and stems in water to create teas, tinctures, or salves for topical use.
